programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: Tom visits Rob Ida's custom car shop, where he checks out his barn-find projects and gets an EXCLUSIVE tour of his one-of-a-kind shop. The best way to explain Rob's shop is not only by calling it a car shop but also an innovation factory. Using the latest technology in metal CNC stretching, 3D printing, and electronic accessories, Rob and his team are setting a new standard for what it means to build custom cars. Follow along as he shows us the latest projects and technology the engineering world has to offer.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: While in St. George, Utah, Tom learns of a junkyard with hundreds of solid old cars, where the owner never sold anything and never allowed anyone inside. Tom makes a few phone calls and sweet-talks his way into Blake's Auto Salvage.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: Tom teams up with his friend Rob Ida to continue the search for cars in New York City. After visiting a few locations, he follows a lead on a late '30s LaSalle, a key piece in a massive collection and one of the best discoveries on the show. Rarely do we come across barn finds in perfect condition that aren't too rusted, yet only need minimal work to get them running. Welcome to this extraordinary collection – 40 barn finds featuring various makes and models, each waiting to be discovered and find new homes.

programme.title: Will It Run?
programme.desc: This Dodge Challenger was parked in the early '90s and hasn't been touched since. Can Davin bring it back to its former glory and get it running and driving again?

programme.title: Will It Run?
programme.desc: Last time we got this iconic 1970 Dodge Challenger TA 340 Six Pack running, but we weren't satisfied with just that. We wanted to see it back on the road again too. (We also wanted to give it a good detailing!)

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: Tom visits Cliff who has a great collection of cars but Tom's main interest is with Cliff's son's 1957 Austin A35. The 1957 Austin A35 was restored by an anonymous person as part of a charity program.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: From his starting point in Midland, Texas, Tom and the Ford wagon cover solid ground and make it to Lubbock, Texas before a grouping of cars held captive behind a chain-link fence capture his attention.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: Tom heads to Great Britain in search of some exotic barn finds and has some great luck at his first location where he discovers a Mercedes-Benz 500SEC AMG and a Jaguar XJS TWR.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: Tom forgoes driving his 1939 Ford Woody Wagon in favor of another car that he's owned for years: his Fern Mist 1953 Ford Ranch Wagon.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: It has always been Tom's dream to open up a barn door and see either a Jaguar XK120 or 140 Roadster sitting inside. Tom gets to relive his dream twice when he finds two Jaguar XK's sitting only a quarter-mile from one another.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: Tom teams up with Davin Reckow of our Redline Rebuild series to uncover some iron in northern Michigan.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter
programme.desc: Tom discovers a warehouse full of rare Brass Era cars and desirable Mid-60s classics that are so packed in that he has to crawl through a Toronado to get to a Corvette.

programme.title: Barn Find Hunter

programme.title: Building the Dream: Colorado
programme.sub-title: This Room Has Unobstrcuted Views of the Rocky Mountain
programme.desc: When you have an unobstructed view of the Rocky Mountains as your backdrop, basic goes out the window. For Houston designer Lucinda Loya, this space is much more than a living room. With a floor-to-ceiling fireplace, gallery art and an oversized sectional this is the perfect spot for working from home, lounging with family, or watching the sunset.

programme.title: Building the Dream: Colorado
programme.sub-title: This Nature-Inspired Dining Room Has Its Own Indoor Swing
programme.desc: This Nature-Inspired Dining Room Has Its Own Indoor Swing

programme.title: Building the Dream: Colorado
programme.sub-title: This Is The World's Most Organized Kitchen
programme.desc: Kitchens should no longer be pretty. Designer Sarah Robertson of New York's Studio Dearborn created a space with function for every culinary level and age group. With handmade tiles, panel fronts on refrigeration and under-counter storage, this kitchen is a multigenerational space with style to spare.

programme.title: Building the Dream: Colorado
programme.sub-title: This is the Outdoor Patio and Fireplace Lounge Area
programme.desc: Tasked with a massive covered area, Portland-based designer Max Humphrey approached it like an indoor great room, dividing this vast space into zones: a living room, an afternoon reading section and of course a ping-pong table neat the eating area. Noz Nozawa is known for many things, but subtlety is not one of them - and we are so grateful for it. For this home, she brought every ounce of her passion for colorful design to the game room and lounge she designed.

programme.title: Building the Dream: Colorado
programme.sub-title: This is the Tea Room and Shed
programme.desc: Houston-based designer Marie Flanigan concocted the ultimate ode to her Southern roots: a room dedicated to tea. With bountiful seating, brass detailing, and red walls ina high-gloss finish, this room is a much-needed quiet moment for one. What busy parents really need is some quality alone time. It was with this in mind that husband-and-wife Brett and Kara Phillips, of High Street Homes, dreamt up a 440-square-foot back house, dubbed "The WeShed." Part indoor hangout space and part covered patio. The perfect place for a getaway without really leaving your home.

programme.title: Holmes on Homes
programme.desc: Dig into a so-called total kitchen reno that turns out to be only skin deep.

programme.title: Holmes on Homes
programme.desc: Explore the downsides of custom building as Mike tours a headache of a house.

programme.title: Holmes On Homes
programme.desc: Jane, a single mom, lives in a typical new home development with her two kids. Since the day they moved into the brand new home, they have been plagued with repeated plumbing and heating issues. During a recent winter cold spell, the porch ceiling caved in, exposin g questionable minimum building code practices and building materials- luckily, Mike and his team have the right answers.

programme.title: Holmes on Homes
programme.desc: This is a story about proximity. Many newly built homes such as semi-detached, town homes and condo units feature designs that share common walls. In the case of our homeowners, Sylvie and Guy, who had just moved into a brand new semi-detached home, they were in for a shock when they realized that they could clearly hear their next door neighbor through the walls! The sound issue was so disruptive that the homeowners were certain moving away was their only option, until Mike and the team found the solution.

programme.title: Treyvaud's Kitchen
programme.desc: Paul shows you in this episode that with simple techniques and perfect seasoning, you can create amazing recipes at home with easy to find ingredients. If you can't find one type of pasta, just use another. If you see something you like, just add it in to your favourite recipe. That's the beauty of food – There are no limitations as to what you can cook!!

programme.title: Treyvaud's Kitchen
programme.desc: Paul shows you how to cook incredible Lamb and Fennel seed skewers A Classic soup with Goats Cheese Scones A surprise little dish using up leftovers and the most amazing rice dish you'll ever see. All this plus loads of fun along the way in this episode of Treyvaud's Kitchen.

programme.title: Treyvaud's Kitchen
programme.desc: If you blink you'll miss the first dish!! The fastest and tastiest cooked salmon recipe. A fantastic Portabello Mushroom recipe that both vegetarians and meat eaters will both love. programme.title: Treyvaud's Kitchen

programme.title: Treyvaud's Kitchen
programme.desc: Paul teaches you the history and the anatomy of Venison before cooking a mouth-watering salad with venison loin, pear and blue cheese. programme.title: Descending

programme.title: Descending

programme.title: Descending
programme.desc: For centuries, the St. Lawrence Seaway and the Great Lakes have shaped the story of Canada. The team takes a trip through history and visits the world's best collection of freshwater shipwrecks to discover what makes this water so great.

programme.title: Descending
programme.desc: In Indonesia, Scott and Ellis experience Raja Ampat – a reserve rich in beauty, diversity and culture. Ellis befriends a local Papuan who teaches him to fish like a local and the divers have a close encounter with Giant Manta Rays. A terrifying accident shakes the confidence of the team.

programme.title: Descending
programme.desc: Following his accident, Scott finds the strength to dive again. In Indonesia's Lembeh Strait, the mission is to film as many strange creatures as they can find. The team concludes their year-long journey on the remote island of Vanuatu to dive one of the greatest shipwrecks in the world and reflect on their underwater adventures.

Schmidt) and Joey (Gino Calabro) in the middle of the desert.

programme.title: The Big Letdown
programme.desc: Froy meets with a lawyer, but his legal troubles are worse than expected. Sonia and Froy confront a debtor who uses a homeless man in his ploy to keep his car. Then, Sonia and Froy discover one debtor's alcoholism is only a farce to cover a bigger secret. And finally, the team's attempt to repo fifteen bikes from a gym is not the morale booster Lou had hoped for.

programme.title: The Waiting Game

Lou and Froy bust some slick moves in an attempt to wrangle a car from a debtor who is prepared to battle. Then, when one woman behaves like a child, Matt puts her in timeout, but worries he may have gone too far. And finally, Lou, Matt, and Froy take on an jealous husband with deadly aim.

Once an area consisting mainly of Cattle farms before it was transformed into a Wildlife Reserve in the early 90's. Today, the Reserve supports a thriving and incredible diversity of Species due to the fact that it lies in an ecological transition zone between the Kalahari and Lowveld Wilderness.

programme.title: The Animal Within
programme.desc: Does the animal kingdom hold the key to immortality? When humans are injured, we have the benefit of medicine and surgery surgeons. But these skills pale in comparison to nature's super healers. Some creatures have evolved to literally regrow parts of themselves.

programme.title: The Animal Within
programme.desc: Humans navigate the world with five senses but we sometimes forget there are aspects of this world we can't see, smell, feel, touch, or taste. But some creatures can sense what humans cannot - from animals like the red fox and sea turtle that use the earth's magnetism to get around, to those who can feel seismic vibrations like elephants.

programme.title: America Over The Edge

programme.title: America Over The Edge

programme.title: Deltas of the World
programme.desc: Hardly any other area is as unspoiled as the Yukon Delta in western Alaska. This fascinating network of swamps and river arms looks like a landscape at the beginning of mankind. Only 25,000 people live in this barren region, which is almost as big as Greece. Most of these inhabitants are indigenous and belong to the ethnic group of the Yupik. They make their living on the steep banks of the Yukon River. And in their villages, built on piles to protect them against flooding, you can see the effects of climate changes on nature - and on the community life of the indigenous population. On the trail of the national fish of Alaska, the salmon, the film follows the Yukon River and follows moose hunters, migratory birds and scientists who do research in the delta.

programme.title: Deltas of the World
programme.desc: South of Barcelona lies the Ebro Delta. Its beaches and lagoons are an exceptional refuge for migratory and native bird species. The delta is known for the numerous pink flamingos that nest near the glittering white saltworks. Sea salt is extracted and rice is cultivated in the delta. The impressive lake landscape is endangered to a certain extent by human intervention. For fear of their harvest, some rice farmers take action against flocks of birds. Invasive rice pests are also a threat to farmers in Europe's largest rice growing region. Other problems make the situation more difficult, such as climate change and rising sea levels or dams holding back sediments. What can be done to preserve the unique Ebro Delta?

Can Santana overcome this distraction as The Beast, Jungle Grrrl, and Tessa set their sights on the Championship Belt that she holds?

programme.title: WOW Women Of Wrestling
programme.desc: The Beast demands her shot at the WOW World Championship. Current Champion Santana Garrett's eyes remain locked on revenge against Tessa Blanchard. And Tessa provides an exclusive interview that explains her reasons for attacking Santana's father. Her response will jar you! The All Natural, Khloe Hurtz seeks revenge against The Governor's Daughter, Abilene Maverick in the main event.

programme.title: WOW Women Of Wrestling
programme.desc: Champion Santana Garrett wants revenge on Tessa Blanchard for what she did to her beloved father. It is a triple threat match that you won't want to miss!

programme.title: WOW Women Of Wrestling
programme.desc: A new Champion in WOW is coming to terms with the fact the bullseye of her competitors is now squarely focused on her. Havok, The Monster Of Madness emerges as a threat to the WOW roster. And Jungle Grrrl continues her hunt for the elusive prize of the World Championship.

programme.title: WOW Women Of Wrestling
programme.desc: Dedicated to her ambition of never quiting, Stephy Slays demonstrates perseverance in an extraordinary match against The Disciplinarian. The smart ending will be unforgettable. Meanwhile, Tessa Blanchard takes her biggest risk since joining WOW.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: Heidi asks the designers to create a red carpet look that she could wear to the Creative Arts Emmys. When Heidi is less than pleased in the workroom, she tells most of the designers to start over. On the Runway, two designers are eliminated.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: For the second Team Challenge of the season, the designers work in pairs to create looks that could be worn for a “nontraditional wedding.” They must design one look for the ceremony, and one for the reception. Dita Von Teese introduces the challenge and serves as guest judge.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: With the help of Chopard, the designers are asked to create an extravagant eveningwear piece inspired by jewelry of their choosing. They also draw inspiration from the Charles James exhibit at the MET, and Tim decides to use his “Tim Gunn Save” on one designer.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: Inspired by the latest Samsung Curved TV, the designers are asked to create avant-garde pieces that look good from every angle. The twist? The pieces also have to walk the Runway…in the rain! The designers have two days to put together a look that can wow the judges and withstand a “Rainway.”

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: After a field trip to the American Girl Store, the designers are asked to create a modern look for a young girl, inspired by the “Beforever” American Doll collection. The designers work with young girls as their models for this challenge.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: For the Mary Kay Challenge, the designers are asked to find a complete stranger on the street to act as their muse (and model) for a makeover. The real women are given a makeup consult, new haircut, and new outfit. One designer runs into trouble with a zipper before the Runway, which leads to a controversial decision.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: The designers are taken to a large warehouse and told that for their next challenge, they will have to bid on storage units in order to obtain their materials. The designers work in teams of two to make a two-piece cohesive collection from the materials they buy. On the second day of their two-day challenge, Tim presents the designers with the task of designing one more look. When the judges cannot decide who to eliminate on the Runway, two designers face off in a one-hour showdown.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.sub-title: Fashion Week: Who's In and Who's Out
programme.desc: The designers are asked to create a “Street Chic” look inspired by a trip around NYC, courtesy of Lexus. They are also thrown a twist…to redesign one of the “losing looks” this season, and their sewing helper is a previously eliminated designer.
